name: Turkey & Poppy Seed Casserole
recipeIngredient: 1 medium onion, chopped (to yield ½ cup)
1 small green bell pepper, chopped (to yield ½ cup)
recipeInstructions:
I use cut-up leftover turkey or chicken (approximately 1 ½ cups).
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Grease a 2-quart casserole dish.
3. Cook and drain noodles as directed on the package.
4. If using ground turkey, melt butter in a 10-inch skillet over medium heat.
5. Cook turkey, onion, and bell pepper for 4 to 6 minutes, stirring frequently,
until turkey is no longer pink. (When using leftover turkey/chicken, cook
just the onion and bell pepper for 4 to 6 minutes. Add turkey/chicken during
the last 2 or 3 minutes.)
6. Stir in tomatoes, salt, and pepper.
7. In a large bowl, mix ricotta, sour cream, and poppy seeds.
8. Stir noodles into the mixture.
9. Spoon three-quarters of the noodle mixture into the casserole dish.
10. Spoon turkey mixture over noodle mixture.
11. Top with remaining noodle mixture.
12. Cover and bake for 50 minutes or until hot.
13. Sprinkle with Romano cheese and parsley.
